name: Luxembourg (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic silver (argent) of the burely field of the historic arms of the Duchy of Luxembourg
the heraldic azure of the burely field
the lion's crown, tongue and claws

description: A burely field of ten horizontal stripes alternating white and blue (white at the top), charged centrally with a red lion rampant double-queued and crowned in yellow, with yellow tongue and claws.
history: Banner of the historic arms of the Duchy of Luxembourg (burely argent and azure of ten pieces, a lion gules crowned and double-queued or, armed and langued or), used as the provincial flag of Belgian Luxembourg since the province's adoption of the design in 2018, replacing an earlier red-white-blue tricolour adopted by the Provincial Council on 3 March 1955. The same arms, in a different rendering, are also borne by the neighbouring Grand Duchy of Luxembourg.

features.symbolism: burely (ten-bar) field of alternating silver and azure, the historic Luxembourg field

features.role: Luxembourg lion, rampant double-queued lion crowned and armed in yellow, with yellow tongue and claws
features.symbolism: the lion of the historic Duchy of Luxembourg, borne over the burely field
